name: Cologne Carnival (Karneval)
description: The biggest and oldest carnival in Germany, known for its Rose Monday parade, giant decorated floats, and focus on mockery, satire, and humor.

name: Munich Carnival (Fasching)
description: Festivities centered on Marienplatz and Viktualienmarkt, featuring the parade of 'Daft Knights' on Carnival Sunday and the Dance of the Market Women on Shrove Tuesday.

name: Mainz Carnival
description: Famous for its performances of satire, literary humor, and mockery of politicians, as well as the largest children's parade in Europe.

name: Düsseldorf Carnival
description: Begins on November 11 with the awakening of the Hoppeditz (Jester) from a mustard pot and concludes with the burning of his effigy.

name: Aachen Carnival
description: Celebrated under the motto 'Spaß an der Freud' (Have fun with joy in your heart).

name: Rhenish Carnival (Karneval)
description: A silly and satirical celebration common in the Rhineland area, characterized by mocking authority and wearing colorful costumes or historical military uniforms.

name: Swabian-Alemannic Carnival (Fastnacht)
description: A more wild and sinister tradition featuring elaborate wooden masks of devils, witches, and animals.

name: Fasching
description: The term used for carnival in Bavaria, Saxony, Berlin, and Brandenburg, featuring traditional masks and the crowning of a prince and princess.
